title: Social Media
description: Modern vocabulary for social media platforms and digital communication

vocabulary.meaning: A person with many followers who can affect opinions online
vocabulary.translation: ng∆∞·ªùi c√≥ ·∫£nh h∆∞·ªüng m·∫°ng x√£ h·ªôi
vocabulary.example: She's a fitness influencer with 2 million followers.

vocabulary.meaning: Content that spreads rapidly across the internet
vocabulary.example: The funny video went viral overnight.

vocabulary.meaning: A word or phrase preceded by # used to categorize content
vocabulary.example: Use the hashtag #TravelGoals to join the conversation.

vocabulary.meaning: A set of rules a platform uses to decide what content to show
vocabulary.example: The Instagram algorithm favors video content.

vocabulary.meaning: Interaction with content through likes, comments and shares
vocabulary.example: High engagement signals popular content to the algorithm.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who produces digital content for online platforms
vocabulary.example: He is a full-time content creator on YouTube.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who subscribes to another's social media account
vocabulary.example: Her follower count grew to 500K in six months.

vocabulary.meaning: The stream of posts shown on a social media homepage
vocabulary.example: The photo appeared at the top of my feed.

vocabulary.meaning: A temporary post visible for 24 hours on platforms like Instagram
vocabulary.example: She posted a behind-the-scenes story on Instagram.

vocabulary.meaning: A short vertical video format on Instagram or Facebook
vocabulary.example: Reels get much more reach than regular posts.

vocabulary.meaning: Text written below a social media post
vocabulary.example: Write an engaging caption to attract more likes.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of earning money from an online channel
vocabulary.example: YouTube monetization requires 1,000 subscribers.

vocabulary.meaning: A paid partnership between a creator and a brand
vocabulary.example: She signed a brand deal with a skincare company.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who posts inflammatory content to provoke others
vocabulary.example: Don't respond to trolls ‚Äî just block them.

vocabulary.meaning: Harassment or bullying carried out through digital technology
vocabulary.example: Cyberbullying causes serious mental health issues.

vocabulary.meaning: False or misleading information spread online
vocabulary.example: Fake news spreads faster than the truth on social media.

vocabulary.meaning: An environment where people only encounter views they already hold
vocabulary.example: Social media creates echo chambers.

vocabulary.meaning: A period of time without using digital devices
vocabulary.example: She took a digital detox for a week on vacation.

vocabulary.meaning: Fraudulent attempts to steal personal information online
vocabulary.example: Never click suspicious links ‚Äî it could be phishing.

vocabulary.meaning: The right to control how your personal data is used
vocabulary.example: Data privacy laws protect users from misuse of personal data.

vocabulary.meaning: Direct Message ‚Äî a private message sent on social media
vocabulary.example: Send me a DM if you're interested in collaborating.

vocabulary.meaning: A video showing someone opening a new product for the first time
vocabulary.example: Her iPhone unboxing video got 3 million views.

vocabulary.meaning: Broadcasting video in real time over the internet
vocabulary.example: He did a live stream to answer fan questions.

vocabulary.meaning: To sign up to receive content from a channel or account
vocabulary.example: Subscribe to my channel for weekly tutorials.

vocabulary.meaning: A humorous image or video that spreads widely online
vocabulary.example: That meme about Mondays was everywhere.

vocabulary.meaning: A digital effect applied to a photo or video
vocabulary.example: She uses a beauty filter in all her selfies.

vocabulary.meaning: A topic gaining rapid popularity on social platforms
vocabulary.example: The topic was trending on Twitter all day.

vocabulary.meaning: An alert from an app about new activity
vocabulary.example: Turn off notifications to focus during study time.

vocabulary.meaning: An online service where content is created and shared
vocabulary.example: TikTok is the fastest-growing social media platform.

vocabulary.meaning: Data about how content performs on a platform
vocabulary.example: Check your analytics to see which posts perform best.

vocabulary.meaning: Misleading content designed to attract clicks
vocabulary.example: Avoid clickbait headlines ‚Äî they damage credibility.

vocabulary.meaning: The area below a post where people can write responses
vocabulary.example: The comment section was filled with positive feedback.

vocabulary.meaning: The total number of times content is displayed
vocabulary.example: The ad got 500,000 impressions in one day.

vocabulary.meaning: The number of unique users who saw your content
vocabulary.example: That post had a reach of 200,000 people.

vocabulary.meaning: An account confirmed as authentic by a platform
vocabulary.example: A blue checkmark means the account is verified.

vocabulary.meaning: An update to how a platform ranks content
vocabulary.example: The algorithm change reduced my reach significantly.

vocabulary.meaning: Working together with other creators on content
vocabulary.example: Their collaboration video went viral.

vocabulary.meaning: A photo taken of oneself using a front-facing camera
vocabulary.example: She posted a gym selfie after her workout.

vocabulary.meaning: A series of connected posts on social media
vocabulary.example: He posted a thread explaining the controversy.

vocabulary.meaning: Restricting content visibility without notifying the user

vocabulary.meaning: To adapt existing content for a different platform or format
vocabulary.example: Repurpose your blog posts as Instagram carousels.

vocabulary.meaning: Creative strategies to rapidly grow a social media audience
vocabulary.example: Cross-promotion is a popular growth hacking strategy.

vocabulary.meaning: A set of rules used to determine content shown to users
vocabulary.example: The algorithm determines which posts appear in your feed.

vocabulary.meaning: Broadcasting video content in real time online

vocabulary.meaning: Brief video content typically under 60 seconds
vocabulary.example: TikTok popularized the short-form video format.

vocabulary.meaning: Short video clips on Instagram
vocabulary.example: She gets the most views from her Instagram Reels.

vocabulary.meaning: A short-video social platform popular with younger audiences
vocabulary.example: Her dance video went viral on TikTok.

vocabulary.meaning: Short vertical videos on YouTube

vocabulary.meaning: A social media post with multiple swipeable images or slides

vocabulary.example: Write a catchy bio to attract followers.

vocabulary.meaning: The image representing you on a social media account
vocabulary.example: Use a professional profile picture on LinkedIn.

vocabulary.meaning: The large banner image at the top of a social media profile
vocabulary.example: Update your cover photo to reflect your brand.

vocabulary.meaning: An alert about activity on your social media account
vocabulary.example: Turn off notifications to reduce digital distraction.

vocabulary.meaning: Controls over who can see your content or profile
vocabulary.example: Check your privacy settings to protect your personal data.

vocabulary.meaning: An incident where private data is accessed without authorization
vocabulary.example: Millions of users were affected by the data breach.

vocabulary.meaning: The trail of data you leave while using the internet
vocabulary.example: Be aware of your digital footprint when posting online.

vocabulary.meaning: A schedule planning what to post and when
vocabulary.example: Use a content calendar to stay consistent.

vocabulary.meaning: Comparing two versions of a post to see which performs better
vocabulary.example: Run A/B testing on your ad creatives to optimise results.

vocabulary.meaning: The percentage of users who take a desired action
vocabulary.example: The campaign doubled the conversion rate.

vocabulary.meaning: The total number of unique users who see your content
vocabulary.example: This post had a reach of 50,000 people.

vocabulary.meaning: The number of times content is displayed, counting repeated views
vocabulary.example: The ad got 100,000 impressions in one day.

vocabulary.meaning: The percentage of users who click a link after seeing it
vocabulary.example: A high click-through rate shows the ad is compelling.

vocabulary.meaning: A professional who manages a brand's social media presence
vocabulary.example: She works as a social media manager for a fashion brand.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who produces digital content for an audience
vocabulary.example: Being a full-time content creator is now a real career.

vocabulary.example: She signed a brand deal with a skincare company.

vocabulary.meaning: The practice of withdrawing support from someone due to offensive behavior
vocabulary.example: The celebrity was a victim of cancel culture after the tweet.

vocabulary.meaning: AI-generated fake video or audio of a real person
vocabulary.example: Deepfake technology poses serious risks to online trust.

vocabulary.meaning: A state where algorithms only show content matching your views
vocabulary.example: Social media filter bubbles reduce exposure to different opinions.

vocabulary.meaning: False or inaccurate information shared without malicious intent
vocabulary.example: Misinformation about vaccines spread rapidly on social media.

vocabulary.meaning: Deliberately false information spread to deceive

vocabulary.meaning: A group of people who interact online around shared interests
vocabulary.example: The gaming online community is one of the largest on Reddit.

vocabulary.meaning: To follow a channel or account to receive updates
vocabulary.example: Subscribe to the channel to get notified of new videos.

vocabulary.meaning: To stop following someone on social media
vocabulary.example: She unfollowed accounts that made her feel negative.

vocabulary.meaning: To prevent a user from seeing your content or contacting you
vocabulary.example: Block toxic users to protect your mental health.

vocabulary.meaning: To stop seeing someone's posts without unfollowing them
vocabulary.example: You can mute an account without them knowing.

vocabulary.meaning: A private message sent between users
vocabulary.example: She received a direct message from the brand.

vocabulary.meaning: Linking another user's account to your post
vocabulary.example: Tagging a brand in your post may earn a repost.

vocabulary.example: The brand reposted her photo to their main account.

vocabulary.meaning: A feature allowing followers to vote on a question
vocabulary.example: She ran a poll asking which product color to launch.

vocabulary.meaning: A period of deliberately avoiding social media
vocabulary.example: She took a two-week social media detox and felt much better.

vocabulary.meaning: Maintaining a healthy relationship with technology
vocabulary.example: Screen time limits support digital wellbeing.

vocabulary.meaning: Advertising content that a brand pays to boost
vocabulary.example: Boost your post with paid promotion to reach a wider audience.

quiz.question: What does 'viral' mean in social media?
What is a 'hashtag'?
options: A type of social media account
A word preceded by # to categorize content

quiz.question: What is 'engagement' in social media?
options: Interaction with content through likes, comments and shares

quiz.question: What is 'cyberbullying'?
options: Harassment carried out through digital technology

quiz.question: What is an 'influencer'?
options: A person with many followers who affects opinions

quiz.question: What is 'phishing'?
options: Fraudulent attempts to steal personal information online

quiz.question: What is 'clickbait'?
options: Misleading content designed to attract clicks

quiz.question: What does 'verified' mean on social media?
options: The account is confirmed as authentic by a platform

quiz.question: What is 'doom scrolling'?
options: Continuously scrolling through negative news or content

quiz.question: What is 'live streaming'?
options: Broadcasting video content in real time

quiz.question: What does 'reach' mean in social media?
options: The depth of a post's content
Total unique users who see your content
How fast a post was shared

quiz.question: What is 'catfishing'?
options: Pretending to be someone else online to deceive others

quiz.question: What is 'de-platforming'?
options: Removing someone from a platform for rule violations

quiz.question: What is 'engagement rate'?
options: The percentage of your audience that interacts with your content
